# UC-14.3.59 · Aranet Probe Battery Percent and Stale Telemetry Gap Detection

## Description

Aranet probes run on primary cells; when battery percentage falls or uplinks stop while gateways remain healthy, environmental compliance dashboards go stale. Combining `battery_pct` with time-since-last-event differentiates dying sensors from broker outages.

## Value

Prevents undetected excursions in GxP or food storage by forcing battery swaps before telemetry gaps exceed SOP limits.

## Implementation

Extract `battery_pct` from Aranet JSON (`battery` or `batteryVoltage` mapped to percent if needed). Ingest at least hourly. Alert when `battery_pct < 15` or no event for >2 hours while peer sensors on the same `gateway_id` continue reporting—indicates dead node versus gateway fault.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Inventory lookup with expected reporting cadence per sensor class.
• Exclude sensors intentionally shelved.

Step 1 — Use `bucket` command for gap detection across irregular MQTT bursts if needed.

Step 2 — Pair with gateway health UC in network category.

Step 3 — Validate by removing battery in lab.

Step 4 — CMMS integration for work orders.

## SPL

```spl
index=environment sourcetype=aranet:sensor sensor_id=*
| stats latest(battery_pct) as batt, latest(_time) as last_seen by sensor_id, location
| eval hours_since_seen=round((now()-last_seen)/3600,2)
| where batt < 15 OR hours_since_seen > 2
| table sensor_id, location, batt, hours_since_seen
| sort hours_since_seen desc
```

## Visualization

Table (sensors stale or low battery), Timeline (gaps), Single value (count at risk).
